### Security Vulnerability: Unbounded Paddle Speed Input\n\n**Description:**\nThe main.py file accepts paddle_speed from the command-line argument. Although the input is checked to ensure it is a digit, there is no upper bound enforced. This allows a user to set paddle_speed to an excessively high value, which can make the game unplayable or crash it (Denial of Service).\n\n**Supporting Information:**\n- The code uses `re.match(r'^\\d+$', user_input)` to validate input but does not restrict the range.\n- Setting paddle_speed to a very large integer (e.g., 1000000) will cause the paddle to move off-screen instantly or freeze the game loop.\n- This is a classic example of missing input bounds, which can be exploited for DoS.\n\n**Recommendation:**\nEnforce a reasonable upper limit for paddle_speed (e.g., between 1 and 20). Reject values outside this range and fall back to a safe default.\n\n---\n*Identified automatically on 05-01-2026 by Research & Development K25 Agent.*
